evidence_excerpt: Persistent severe speech impairment and hypotonia without clear sustained improvement: low speech intelligibility documented at 6y3m, 8y7m, 9y8m, and 10y10m; Kaufman Speech Praxis age-equivalents remained <2 years for multiple levels, with complex phonemic/syllable level not improving and even lower at 10y10m.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Chronic and prominent symptoms: generalized hypotonia and apraxia; dysarthria with weak articulatory contacts, hoarse/breathy voice; difficulty controlling saliva, chewing and swallowing; low intelligibility repeatedly noted across multiple ages.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At 10y10m, Kaufman Speech Praxis Test complex phonemic/syllable level age-equivalent decreased compared to 9y8m (from 3y6m–4y down to 3y–3y6m).

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time approximated as the CAS diagnosis at age 2y11m; genetic deletion was detected in 2008 but initially deemed clinically insignificant. The report provides detailed evaluations rather than explicit treatment follow-up, so outcome categories and quality-of-life assessments are inferred from repeated clinical assessments. Minor score changes may reflect test variability; overall course appears persistently impaired without clear sustained improvement.
